title: 9 Herbal Essential Oils For Psoriasis
intro_desc: Many women suffer from psoriasis, a condition that makes their skin red, itchy, and scaly. Hormonal changes, stress, and genetics can trigger psoriasis in females. This can lead to low self-esteem, anxiety, and depression. Women with psoriasis may feel self-conscious about their appearance, avoiding social events, and intimate relationships. They may also experience pain, discomfort, and bleeding from cracked skin, making daily activities challenging. Some women may have to miss work or school due to their condition, affecting their career and education. Psoriasis can also make it difficult for women to manage their periods, as the condition can cause heavy bleeding and painful cramps. Female psoriasis sufferers often feel frustrated, isolated, and disappointed by their condition. Despite these challenges, many women with psoriasis are eager to find natural remedies to manage their symptoms. This is where herbal essential oils come in - a natural and gentle way to help soothe, calm, and heal the skin. These oils can be used in a diffuser, applied topically, or added to bath water to promote relaxation, reduce inflammation, and promote healthy skin cell growth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis essential oils contains anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help alleviate symptoms of psoriasis. The oil's high concentration of triterpenoids and carotenoids, specifically beta-carotene and lutein, may help reduce redness and inflammation. These compounds have been shown to have a soothing effect on the skin, which can help to calm the skin's immune response and reduce the appearance of psoriasis symptoms. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of the oil can help to prevent infections that can exacerbate psoriasis. If you're considering using Calendula officinalis essential oils for psoriasis, start by diluting a few drops of the oil with a carrier oil and applying it to the affected areas. You can also add the oil to your bath water for a relaxing and therapeutic soak. Be sure to do a patch test before using the oil extensively to ensure you're not allergic to it. As you start using Calendula officinalis essential oils, you may notice a reduction in redness and inflammation, and a decrease in the thickness and scaling of the skin. Your skin may feel softer and more comfortable, and the appearance of psoriasis symptoms may begin to fade. However, it's essential to remember that everyone's skin is different, and results may vary. It's also crucial to consult with a healthcare professional or dermatologist before using essential oils, especially if you're already taking medications or have a severe case of psoriasis.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal essential oils for psoriasis

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica essential oils contains compounds that help with psoriasis by reducing inflammation and itching. The plant's properties include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, thanks to constituents like isoorientin, isovitexin, and kaempferol. These compounds help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and itching associated with psoriasis. To use Urtica dioica essential oils for psoriasis, mix a few drops with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and apply it topically to the affected area. You can also add it to a warm bath for a relaxing and soothing experience.  When using Urtica dioica essential oils, you may notice a decrease in inflammation and itching within a few days to a week. The skin may start to feel smoother and look less red, and the patches may begin to heal. However, it's essential to note that everyone's skin is different, and results may vary. If you have severe psoriasis or are considering using Urtica dioica essential oils as a treatment, consult with a healthcare professional or a certified aromatherapist for guidance.
remedies.image_alt: Urtica dioica herbal essential oils for psoriasis

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum essential oils contains compounds like flavonoids, terpenoids, and sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help with psoriasis symptoms. These properties can reduce redness, itching, and scaling associated with the condition. The constituents, including silicic acid and silymarin, have been shown to have a soothing effect on the skin, promoting healing and reducing inflammation.  To use Silybum marianum essential oils for psoriasis, mix a few drops with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and apply it topically to the affected areas. It's recommended to do a patch test first to ensure you're not allergic to the oil. Start with small amounts and gradually increase as needed. You can also add a few drops to your bath water for a relaxing and therapeutic soak.  When using Silybum marianum essential oils for psoriasis, you can expect to see a reduction in symptoms like redness, itching, and scaling. The oil may also help to promote healing and reduce inflammation, allowing the skin to recover and become smoother. Regular use may also help to prevent future outbreaks. However, it's essential to note that everyone's skin is different, and results may vary. It's also crucial to consult with a healthcare professional or dermatologist before using any new essential oils, especially if you're already taking medications or have underlying health conditions.
remedies.image_alt: Silybum marianum herbal essential oils for psoriasis

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis essential oils contains anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help alleviate psoriasis symptoms. The oil's main constituents, such as aloe-emodin, aloin, and aloe-vera, have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. These compounds can help to calm red, itchy, and scaly skin, making it a popular natural remedy for psoriasis sufferers.  To use Aloe barbadensis essential oil for psoriasis, apply a few drops to the affected area after bathing or showering, when the skin is still damp. You can also add a few drops to your bath water for an intense soak. Mix the oil with a carrier oil, like coconut or jojoba oil, before applying it to the skin to avoid any potential irritation. When using the oil, you may start to notice improvements in the texture and appearance of your skin within a few days.  As the inflammation and redness in the affected areas decrease, you may notice a reduction in itching and the scaling of the skin. The oil's soothing properties can also help to reduce the appearance of psoriasis plaques and promote healthy skin cell growth. However, it's essential to note that everyone's skin is different, and results may vary. It's also crucial to consult with a healthcare professional before using essential oils, especially if you have sensitive skin or are taking any medications.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal essential oils for psoriasis

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils contains compounds like cineole, limonene, and terpinen-4-ol, which have antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. These properties can help with psoriasis by reducing inflammation, preventing bacterial and fungal infections, and soothing irritated skin. The antiseptic properties in Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils can help control the growth of bacteria and fungi that can exacerbate psoriasis symptoms.  To use Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils for psoriasis, mix a few drops with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and apply it topically to the affected area. You can also add a few drops to your bath water for a relaxing and soothing experience. When using essential oils, make sure to do a patch test on a small area of skin to check for any adverse reactions. When using Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils for psoriasis, you may notice a reduction in inflammation and redness, and an improvement in skin texture and appearance. The antiseptic properties may also help prevent infections and promote wound healing. However, it's essential to note that everyone's skin is different, and what works for one person may not work for another. If you have severe psoriasis or experience any adverse reactions, consult a healthcare professional for guidance.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal essential oils for psoriasis

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils contains properties and constituents that can help alleviate the symptoms of psoriasis. The antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties in the oil, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, can help reduce redness and swelling associated with psoriasis. The calming effects of the oil can also help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are known to exacerbate psoriasis symptoms. To use Lavandula angustifolia essential oils for psoriasis, dilute a few drops of the oil in a carrier oil and apply it topically to the affected areas. You can also add a few drops to your bath water for a relaxing and soothing experience. When applying the oil, gently massage it into the skin to increase blood flow and promote healing. As you take these actions, you may notice a reduction in redness and inflammation, and an improvement in skin texture and appearance. The oil may also help to reduce the frequency and severity of psoriasis flare-ups, allowing you to manage your symptoms more effectively.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oils for psoriasis

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum essential oils contains compounds like hyperforin and hypericin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate psoriasis symptoms. Psoriasis is a skin condition characterized by red, scaly patches, and inflammation. The essential oil's properties may help reduce inflammation and soothe the skin, making it a potential natural remedy for psoriasis.  To try Hypericum perforatum essential oils for psoriasis, you can mix a few drops of the oil with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and apply it topically to the affected areas. Be sure to do a patch test before using it extensively to ensure you don't have any sensitivity. You can also add a few drops to your bath water for a relaxing and soothing experience.  When using Hypericum perforatum essential oils for psoriasis, you may notice a reduction in redness and inflammation, and an improvement in skin texture. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties may also help reduce the severity of psoriasis symptoms, allowing you to manage the condition more effectively. However, results may vary, and it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using essential oils, especially if you're already taking medications or have a severe case of psoriasis.
remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal essential oils for psoriasis

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea essential oils contains compounds like cichoric acid, echinacoside, and sesquiterpenes that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties can help reduce redness and swelling associated with psoriasis. The essential oils may also help promote wound healing and reduce scarring by increasing blood flow and collagen production. The sesquiterpenes in Echinacea purpurea essential oils, particularly alpha-humulene and beta-caryophyllene, have shown to have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help soothe the skin and reduce psoriasis symptoms. To use Echinacea purpurea essential oils for psoriasis, dilute a few drops in a carrier oil like coconut or jojoba oil, and apply it topically to the affected areas. You can also add a few drops to your bath water for a relaxing and soothing soak. Before applying the essential oils, do a patch test to check for any skin irritation or allergic reactions. Mix a small amount of the essential oils with a carrier oil and apply it to a discreet area of your skin, like behind your ear or on your wrist, and wait 24 hours to see if any adverse reactions occur. If you experience any redness, itching, or burning after applying the essential oils, discontinue use and consult with a healthcare professional. However, if you notice improvements in your skin, such as reduced redness, scaling, or itching, continue to use the essential oils as directed. It's essential to note that Echinacea purpurea essential oils may not completely cure psoriasis, but it can help manage symptoms and promote healing. Regular use of the essential oils may also help reduce the frequency and severity of psoriasis flare-ups.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal essential oils for psoriasis

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera essential oils contains compounds like aloe-emodin, aloin, and vitamins A, C, and E, which help reduce inflammation and promote skin health. These properties make it an effective remedy for psoriasis, a condition characterized by red, scaly patches on the skin. The anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties of aloe vera essential oils help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and itching associated with psoriasis.  If you're considering using aloe vera essential oils for psoriasis, start by diluting a few drops of the oil with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Apply the mixture to the affected area 2-3 times a day, gently massaging it into the skin. You can also add a few drops of aloe vera essential oil to your bath water for a relaxing and rejuvenating soak.  When you take these actions, you may notice a reduction in inflammation and redness, as well as a decrease in the severity of psoriasis symptoms. The antiseptic properties of aloe vera essential oils may also help prevent infections and promote faster healing. Be patient, as it may take some time to see noticeable improvements.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe vera herbal essential oils for psoriasis

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help ease symptoms of psoriasis, but it's unlikely to prevent it from forming in the first place. Some herbal teas, like chamomile and peppermint, have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe skin irritation. However, psoriasis is a complex condition that requires a comprehensive approach to manage.
faq_safe: Herbal teas for psoriasis can be a good option, but safety depends on the type and how often you drink them. Some herbal teas, like chamomile and peppermint, are generally considered safe. However, teas containing substances like aloe vera or turmeric may cause stomach issues or interact with medications if consumed daily.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can take a few weeks to a few months to show results in psoriasis. Some people may notice improvement in their skin after 2-3 weeks, while others may take 2-3 months to see noticeable changes. Consistency is key, so drinking herbal teas regularly can help manage symptoms.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for psoriasis can help soothe symptoms. Some people find it best to drink it in the morning, as it can help set a calm tone for the day. This might help reduce stress and inflammation that can trigger psoriasis flare-ups.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for psoriasis. Some herbs, like St. John's Wort, may affect how well the medication works or increase the risk of side effects. Other herbs, like ginkgo biloba, can thin the blood and make it harder for the body to heal skin lesions. This can affect the treatment's effectiveness.
faq_pregnant: It's best to be cautious when drinking herbal teas for psoriasis while pregnant. Some herbs can affect the baby's growth or cause other problems. For example, dandelion and sassafras teas may not be safe, while peppermint and chamomile teas are often considered okay in moderation. Always check the ingredients carefully before drinking.
faq_breastfeeding: If you have psoriasis and are breastfeeding, you can try herbal teas for relief. However, it's essential to be cautious. Some herbal teas, like chamomile and peppermint, are considered safe for breastfeeding. But others, like goldenseal and evening primrose, might affect milk supply or baby's health. Always choose teas with proven safety for breastfeeding.
